en: find out how he lost his voice box.
ko: 그의 목소리 상자를 잃은 이유를 알아보자. 여기 뭐가 있지? 그의 이름은 바클리야. 정말 기쁘네.

en: What have we here?
His name is Barkley.
It's delightful.
It's not delightful, it's disgusting.
Look at Warhol, look at Coombs.
A lot of great works of contemporary art were initially dismissed as garbage.
ko: 많은 현대 미술 작품들이 처음에는 쓰레기로 치부되었어.

en: Excellent point, Trish.
This is not even garbage. They wouldn't take it.
ko: 그들은 이걸 받아들이지 않을 거야. 너무 커서 쓰레기통에 들어가지 않아.

en: It's too big for the can.
Now, what I see is a man who is not a man.
ko: 내가 보는 것은 인간이 아닌 남자야.

en: He is a servant who has lost his dignity.
It is a commentary on the class system in society, as well as a handy place to put your keys and sunglasses.
ko: 사회의 계급 시스템에 대한 논평이자, 열쇠와 선글라스를 두기 좋은 장소야.

en: Whatever he is, your Barkley is going back to the attic.
ko: 그가 무엇이든, 너의 바클리는 다락방으로 돌아갈 거야.

en: I'm going to see the baby.
Oh, Barkley. Very clever.
Come on guys, let's play.
ko: 제스처 게임. 나와 트리시가 아빠와 제이와 대결할 거야. 경고할게, 제이, 이 둘은 정말 잘해.

en: What are you going to play? Charades.
Me and Trish against Dad and Jay.
I have to warn you, Jay, these two are very good.
ko: 마치 그들만의 언어가 있는 것 같아. 나도 놀고 싶어. 어느 팀에 들어가야 해?

en: It's like they have their own language.
I want to play too.
Which team should I join?
ko: 맨니와 놀아. 다섯 단어야, 책이야. 배에 노인이 있어. 그는 큰 물고기를 잡았어.

en: Play with Manny.
Five words, it's a book.
There's an old man on the boat.
He caught a big fish.
